channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Revisits a Pirate Classic in July
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has officially announced the Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced release date, and you can expect to revisit the classic pirate adventure with all-new visuals and extra stuff to do in July.

Resynced was one of the industry's worst-kept secrets until it was officially confirmed by Ubisoft at the start of last month, and now, the publisher has released a first look at what you can expect the remade swashbuckling sailing sim to look like (and this is very much a remake, not a remaster).

Admittedly, the first major trailer for Resynced doesn't really feature much in the way of gameplay. Ubisoft itself says at the outset that the trailer features a "mix of work-in-progress gameplay and in-game cinematics", but it's more of a teaser than a full reveal.

As part of Ubisoft's promise to remake Black Flag rather than just remastering it, Resynced won't just feature improved visuals and performance; it'll also have brand new questlines built around familiar faces like Stede Bonnet and Blackbeard.

New features like pets and a photo mode are also being added, alongside adjustments to core systems like combat, stealth, and parkour to make them smoother and address "previous pain points".

The idea of remaking Assassin's Creed's most popular title probably makes sense from a business standpoint. Ubisoft isn't exactly riding high right now, and so a quick win powered by nostalgia for a series that's become divisive (if still commercially successful) is logical.

It also doesn't look like Resynced will espouse any of the RPG elements that have made recent Assassin's Creed games such points of contention among fans. This is very much a reimagined version of the 2013 adventure, and I'm very much here for it on that front.

Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced launches on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S (sorry, Switch 2 owners) on July 9th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Conan Exiles Enhanced Is a Huge Visual Upgrade Coming to Steam in May
item.content:encoded:
Funcom has announced that Conan Exiles is getting a big visual upgrade on Steam in May, with Conan Exiles Enhanced bringing an engine update, improved visuals, and enhanced performance, plus a couple of surprising gameplay additions.

Enhanced will switch the game's engine from Unreal Engine 4 to 5, which Funcom says will also enable Conan Exiles to target 60 frames per second across all of its graphical presets "while also delivering strong performance on Steam Deck".

Naturally, the engine change will revitalize many of Conan Exiles' environments, bringing them up to speed with modern visual standards, but Funcom says it should also result in better performance. Given some players' issues with Unreal Engine 5 stutters, your mileage may vary, but let's hope for the best.

Lest you think it's just visual and performance upgrades, though, think again, because Conan Exiles Enhanced is also bringing a much-needed change to the way travel between the Exiled Lands and the Isle of Siptah expansion area works.

Once Enhanced arrives, you'll be able to travel freely between both areas, so you no longer need to choose between them when your game starts. Multiplayer servers can also support both maps now.

The update will also introduce the ability to host multiple characters on each account, and the user interface for Conan Exiles will be revamped as well, so it should be a little easier to find your way around. Naturally, saves can be transferred between the original game and Enhanced.

For now, Conan Exiles Enhanced is only available on Steam, but Funcom says it's considering bringing the update to other platforms as well. You can check out the announcement post here to learn more about Enhanced.

Conan Exiles is available now for PC, PlayStation 4, and Xbox, and it's playable on PS5 via backwards compatibility as well. The Enhanced update lands on PC via Steam on May 5th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
